title: Leucasia zeylanica
taxonomy: Leucasia zeylanica is classified under the family Annonaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. This family includes approximately 130 genera and 2,000 species. The genus Leucasia is a part of the Annonaceae family and contains a few species found in tropical regions. Leucasia zeylanica, specifically, is a species of flowering plant native to Sri Lanka. The classification of Leucasia zeylanica is further divided into its botanical name, which consists of genus and species names.

benefits: Leucasia zeylanica is a plant species native to Sri Lanka, and it has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and stems are used to treat fever, cough, and cold, as well as to reduce inflammation. properties: Leucasia zeylanica, also known as Ceylon lead tree, possesses several therapeutic properties, primarily due to its bioactive compounds. intro: Leucasia zeylanica, also known as Indian Pennywort or Gotu Kola, is a perennial plant native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in India and Sri Lanka. The health benefits of Leucasia zeylanica include its ability to improve cognitive function, enhance memory, and boost antioxidant defenses, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Leucasia zeylanica is commonly used in traditional medicine and is available in various herbal preparations, including teas, capsules, and extracts, often standardized to contain 30% to 40% triterpenoid saponins.
